rationale: The citing paper’s core method (stock-return response models linking brand metrics, unanticipated earnings, and abnormal returns, with a high-tech subsample) closely matches the cited paper’s framework and findings on brand-attitude shocks, future ROE, and abnormal stock returns in high-technology markets. Given this strong construct/method overlap and the cited paper’s role as an early value-relevance test for brand attitude, this looks plausibly foundational or a direct comparison point rather than an incidental mention.
